full_source: Lecture on speech processing, HMM, MFCC, cepstrum, stochastic, filterbank, Whisper, Wav2Vec, CTC, transformer, code switching. Lecture on speech processing, HMM, MFCC, cepstrum, stochastic, filterbank, Wav2Vec. How do you make decisions when facing a high stakes choice with no clear precedence, a lot of uncertainty, having to balance input? There are many factors to my decision making. First, I'm perhaps the only politician in India who has stayed overnight in around eighty-five to ninety percent of the districts across the country. This was before my current role. I used to travel extensively. I learned a lot from those experiences. They gave me first-hand knowledge of the ground realities and grassroots level issues, not something asked or heard or learned merely from books. Secondly, from a governance perspective, I carry no baggage of any kind. I don't carry any baggage that weighs me down or forces me to act a certain way. Thirdly, I have a simple yardstick for decisions. My country first. I always question if what I'm doing harms my nation in any way. Additionally, Mahatma Gandhi once said that if you're ever unsure when making a decision, think of the poorest person's face, remember them, and ask yourself, will this help them? Then your decision will be right. That wisdom always guides me, remembering ordinary citizens and considering how my actions affect them. Another factor in my approach is that I'm very well connected. In my administration, my officials know this well and probably feel overwhelmed by it. By the fact that my information channels are numerous and are very active. Because of that, I receive lots of insights from various sources. So, when someone comes to brief me, that's not my only source of information. I always have additional perspectives available to me. Another thing, I maintain a learner's mindset. Suppose I'm not familiar with something and an official explains it to me. I approach them like a student and ask, can you clarify this? How does it work? Then what happens next and how? And whenever I have different information, I deliberately play devil's advocate and ask challenging questions. I thoroughly analyze the issue from multiple angles, hoping that careful evaluation will yield something valuable. Then, once I converge toward a decision or action that is worth taking, I share the idea informally with like-minded people, just to gauge their reactions and see how they respond, gathering insights and feedback before moving forward. Until finally, I have a strong conviction that my decision is right. This entire decision-making process doesn't actually take much time. My speed is very fast. Let me share an example. How did I make decisions during COVID? I had Nobel Prize winners advising me, giving countless economic examples from around the world. They'd say, if this country is doing this, that country did that, you should do it too. Renowned economists constantly bombarded me with suggestions. Political parties pressured me relentlessly, urging me to spend huge amounts of money. But I didn't act immediately. I paused and reflected. What exactly should I do? Then, considering the unique conditions of my own country, I made a clear decision. I wouldn't let the poor sleep hungry. I wouldn't allow social tensions to arise over basic daily needs. These core principles guided my approach. The entire world was in lockdown. Global economies were collapsing. Everyone pressured me to empty the treasury, print more currency, and flood money everywhere. But I decided that this was not the right economic route to follow. And so instead, the path I chose, after carefully listening to experts, understanding their opinions without opposing them, and combining their advice with my own country's situation and my personal experiences, created a system that worked effectively. As a result, when the whole world suffered from severe inflation immediately after COVID, India did not. Today, my country is steadily advancing at a rapid pace, emerging as one of the fastest-growing major economies in the world. The main reason is that during that crisis, with patience and discipline, I resisted the temptation to apply every global theory blindly. We didn't worry about...

How do अहाँ make decisions when facing a high stakes choice संग नहिं clear precedence, a lot केर uncertainty, having कें balance input? ओतए छथि बहुत factors कें my decision making. First, I'm perhaps the only politician मे India who has stayed overnight मे around eighty-five कें ninety प्रतिशत केर the districts across the country. ई छल पहिने my current role. हम used कें travel extensively. हम सीखब a lot सँ those experiences. ओ सभ gave me first-hand knowledge केर the ground realities आ grassroots level issues, नहिं something asked वा heard वा सीखब merely सँ books. Secondly, सँ a governance perspective, हम carry नहिं baggage केर any kind. हम don't carry any baggage ओ weighs me down वा forces me कें act a certain way. Thirdly, हम have a simple yardstick लेल decisions. My country first. हम always प्रश्न if what I'm doing harms my nation मे any way. Additionally, Mahatma Gandhi once said ओ if you're ever unsure when making a decision, think केर the poorest person's face, remember them, आ ask yourself, -त ई help them? Then your decision -त be right. ओ wisdom always guides me, remembering ordinary citizens आ विचार करू how my actions affect them. Another factor मे my दृष्टिकोण अछि ओ I'm बड्ड well connected. मे my administration, my officials know ई well आ probably feel overwhelmed by it. By the fact ओ my सूचना channels छथि numerous आ छथि बड्ड active. किएक तँ केर that, हम receive lots केर insights सँ various sources. So, when someone comes कें brief me, that's नहिं my only source केर information. हम always have additional perspectives available कें me. Another thing, हम maintain a learner's mindset. Suppose I'm नहिं familiar संग something आ an official बुझाएब it कें me. हम दृष्टिकोण them like a student आ ask, can अहाँ clarify this? How does it work? Then what happens आगाँ आ how? आ whenever हम have different information, हम deliberately play devil's advocate आ ask challenging questions. हम thoroughly analyze the issue सँ multiple angles, hoping ओ careful evaluation -त yield something valuable. Then, once हम converge toward a decision वा action ओ अछि worth taking, हम share the idea informally संग like-minded people, just कें gauge their reactions आ देखब how ओ सभ respond, gathering insights आ feedback पहिने moving forward. Until finally, हम have a strong conviction ओ my decision अछि right. ई entire decision-making प्रसंस्करण doesn't actually take much time. My speed अछि बड्ड fast. Let me share an example. How did हम make decisions समयमे COVID? हम had Nobel Prize winners advising me, giving countless economic उदाहरण सँ around the world. They'd say, if ई country अछि doing this, ओ country did that, अहाँ should do it too. Renowned economists constantly bombarded me संग suggestions. Political parties pressured me relentlessly, urging me कें spend huge amounts केर money. मुदा हम didn't act immediately. हम paused आ reflected. What exactly should हम do? Then, विचार करू the unique conditions केर my own country, हम made a clear decision. हम wouldn't let the poor sleep hungry. हम wouldn't allow social tensions कें arise ऊपर basic daily needs. These core principles guided my approach. The entire world छल मे lockdown. Global economies were collapsing. Everyone pressured me कें empty the treasury, print बेसी currency, आ flood money everywhere. मुदा हम decided ओ ई छल नहिं the right economic route कें follow. आ so instead, the path हम chose, बाद carefully listening कें experts, बुझब their opinions without opposing them, आ combining their advice संग my own country's situation आ my personal experiences, created a तंत्र ओ worked effectively. As a result, when the whole world suffered सँ severe inflation immediately बाद COVID, India did not. Today, my country अछि steadily advancing at a rapid pace, emerging as एक केर the fastest-growing major economies मे the world. The main reason अछि ओ समयमे ओ crisis, संग patience आ discipline, हम resisted the temptation कें लागू करब every global theory blindly. हम didn't worry about...

segments.source_text: How do you make decisions when facing a high stakes choice with no clear precedence, a lot of uncertainty, having to balance input?

segments.source_text: There are many factors to my decision making.

segments.source_text: First, I'm perhaps the only politician in India who has stayed overnight in around eighty-five to ninety percent of the districts across the country.

segments.maithili: First, I'm perhaps the only politician मे India who has stayed overnight मे around eighty-five कें ninety प्रतिशत केर the districts across the country.

segments.source_text: This was before my current role. I used to travel extensively.
segments.source_ipa: ðɪs wɑːz bɪfɔːr maɪ kɝʌnt rələ. aɪ juːzd tuː trævʌl əxtənsəvəly.
segments.maithili: ई छल पहिने my current role. हम used कें travel extensively.

segments.source_text: I learned a lot from those experiences. They gave me first-hand knowledge of the ground realities and grassroots level issues, not something asked or heard or learned merely from books.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ lɝnd ʌ lɑːt frʌm ðoʊz əxpərəəncəs. ðeɪ ɡeɪv miː fərst-hənd nɑːlʌdʒ ʌv t̪ʰeː ɡraʊnd riːælʌtiːz ʌnd ɡræsruːts lɛvʌl əssəəs, nɑːt sʌmθɪŋ æskt ɔːr hɝd ɔːr lɝnd mɪrliː frʌm buːks.
segments.maithili: हम सीखब a lot सँ those experiences. ओ सभ gave me first-hand knowledge केर the ground realities आ grassroots level issues, नहिं something asked वा heard वा सीखब merely सँ books.

segments.source_text: Secondly, from a governance perspective, I carry no baggage of any kind.
segments.source_ipa: səcəndly, frʌm ʌ ɡʌvɝnʌns pərspəctəvə, aɪ kæriː noʊ bæɡʌdʒ ʌv ɛniː kənd.
segments.maithili: Secondly, सँ a governance perspective, हम carry नहिं baggage केर any kind.

segments.source_text: I don't carry any baggage that weighs me down or forces me to act a certain way.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ doʊnt kæriː ɛniː bæɡʌdʒ ðæt weɪz miː daʊn ɔːr fɔːrsɪz miː tuː ækt ʌ sɝtʌn weɪ.
segments.maithili: हम don't carry any baggage ओ weighs me down वा forces me कें act a certain way.

segments.source_text: Thirdly, I have a simple yardstick for decisions. My country first. I always question if what I'm doing harms my nation in any way.
segments.source_ipa: ðərdly, aɪ hæv ʌ sɪmpʌl jɑːrdstɪk fɔːr dəcəʒəns. maɪ kʌntriː fərst. aɪ ɔːlweɪz kwɛstʃʌn ɪf wʌt aɪm duːɪŋ hɑːrmz maɪ neɪʃʌn ɪn ɛniː weɪ.
segments.maithili: Thirdly, हम have a simple yardstick लेल decisions. My country first. हम always प्रश्न if what I'm doing harms my nation मे any way.

segments.source_text: Additionally, Mahatma Gandhi once said that if you're ever unsure when making a decision, think of the poorest person's face, remember them, and ask yourself, will this help them?

segments.source_text: Then your decision will be right. That wisdom always guides me, remembering ordinary citizens and considering how my actions affect them.

segments.maithili: Then your decision -त be right. ओ wisdom always guides me, remembering ordinary citizens आ विचार करू how my actions affect them.

segments.source_text: Another factor in my approach is that I'm very well connected. In my administration, my officials know this well and probably feel overwhelmed by it.

segments.maithili: हम दृष्टिकोण them like a student आ ask, can अहाँ clarify this? How does it work? Then what happens आगाँ आ how?

segments.source_text: And whenever I have different information, I deliberately play devil's advocate and ask challenging questions.

segments.maithili: आ whenever हम have different information, हम deliberately play devil's advocate आ ask challenging questions.

segments.source_text: I thoroughly analyze the issue from multiple angles, hoping that careful evaluation will yield something valuable.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ θɝoʊliː ænʌlaɪz t̪ʰeː ɪʃuː frʌm mʌltʌpʌl əŋləs, hoʊpɪŋ ðæt kɛrfʌl ɪvæljuːeɪʃʌn wɪl jiːld sʌmθɪŋ vələəblə.
segments.maithili: हम thoroughly analyze the issue सँ multiple angles, hoping ओ careful evaluation -त yield something valuable.

segments.source_text: Then, once I converge toward a decision or action that is worth taking, I share the idea informally with like-minded people,
segments.source_ipa: ðən, wʌns aɪ kʌnvɝdʒ tʌwɔːrd ʌ dɪsɪʒʌn ɔːr ækʃʌn ðæt ɪz wɝθ təkəŋ, aɪ ʃɛr t̪ʰeː aɪdiːʌ ɪnfɔːrmʌliː wɪð ləkə-məndəd pəəplə,
segments.maithili: Then, once हम converge toward a decision वा action ओ अछि worth taking, हम share the idea informally संग like-minded people,

segments.source_text: just to gauge their reactions and see how they respond, gathering insights and feedback before moving forward.
segments.source_ipa: dʒʌst tuː ɡeɪdʒ ðɛr riːækʃʌnz ʌnd siː haʊ ðeɪ rəspənd, ɡæðɝɪŋ ɪnsaɪts ʌnd fiːdbæk bɪfɔːr muːvɪŋ fərwərd.
segments.maithili: just कें gauge their reactions आ देखब how ओ सभ respond, gathering insights आ feedback पहिने moving forward.

segments.source_text: Until finally, I have a strong conviction that my decision is right.
segments.source_ipa: ʌntɪl fənəlly, aɪ hæv ʌ strɔːŋ kʌnvɪkʃʌn ðæt maɪ dɪsɪʒʌn ɪz raɪt.
segments.maithili: Until finally, हम have a strong conviction ओ my decision अछि right.

segments.source_text: This entire decision-making process doesn't actually take much time. My speed is very fast.
segments.source_ipa: ðɪs ɪntaɪɝ dəcəʒən-məkəŋ prɑːsɛs dʌzʌnt æktʃʌwʌliː teɪk mʌtʃ təmə. maɪ spiːd ɪz vɛriː fəst.
segments.maithili: ई entire decision-making प्रसंस्करण doesn't actually take much time. My speed अछि बड्ड fast.

segments.source_text: Let me share an example. How did I make decisions during COVID?
segments.source_ipa: lɛt miː ʃɛr æn əxəmplə. haʊ dɪd aɪ meɪk dɪsɪʒʌnz dʊrɪŋ cəvəd?
segments.maithili: Let me share an example. How did हम make decisions समयमे COVID?

segments.source_text: I had Nobel Prize winners advising me, giving countless economic examples from around the world.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ hæd noʊbɛl praɪz wɪnɝz ædvaɪzɪŋ mə, ɡɪvɪŋ kaʊntlʌs ɛkʌnɑːmɪk ɪɡzæmpʌlz frʌm ɝaʊnd t̪ʰeː wərld.
segments.maithili: हम had Nobel Prize winners advising me, giving countless economic उदाहरण सँ around the world.

segments.source_text: They'd say, if this country is doing this, that country did that, you should do it too.
segments.source_ipa: ðeɪd seɪ, ɪf ðɪs kʌntriː ɪz duːɪŋ ðəs, ðæt kʌntriː dɪd ðət, juː ʃʊd ɖoː ɪt ʈoː.
segments.maithili: They'd say, if ई country अछि doing this, ओ country did that, अहाँ should do it too.

segments.source_text: Renowned economists constantly bombarded me with suggestions.
segments.source_ipa: rɪnaʊnd ɪkɑːnʌmɪsts kɑːnstʌntliː bɑːmbɑːrdɪd miː wɪð səggəsʃəns.
segments.maithili: Renowned economists constantly bombarded me संग suggestions.

segments.source_text: Political parties pressured me relentlessly, urging me to spend huge amounts of money.
segments.source_ipa: pʌlɪtʌkʌl pɑːrtiːz prɛʃɝd miː rələntləssly, ɝdʒɪŋ miː tuː spɛnd hjuːdʒ ʌmaʊnts ʌv mənəy.
segments.maithili: Political parties pressured me relentlessly, urging me कें spend huge amounts केर money.

segments.source_text: But I didn't act immediately. I paused and reflected. What exactly should I do?
segments.source_ipa: bʌt aɪ dɪdʌnt ækt əmmədəətəly. aɪ pɔːzd ʌnd rəfləctəd. wʌt ɪɡzæktliː ʃʊd aɪ ɖoː?
segments.maithili: मुदा हम didn't act immediately. हम paused आ reflected. What exactly should हम do?

segments.source_text: Then, considering the unique conditions of my own country, I made a clear decision.
segments.source_ipa: ðən, kʌnsɪdɝɪŋ t̪ʰeː juːniːk kʌndɪʃʌnz ʌv maɪ oʊn caʊntry, aɪ meɪd ʌ klɪr dəcəʒən.
segments.maithili: Then, विचार करू the unique conditions केर my own country, हम made a clear decision.

segments.source_text: I wouldn't let the poor sleep hungry.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ wʊdʌnt lɛt t̪ʰeː pʊr sliːp həŋry.
segments.maithili: हम wouldn't let the poor sleep hungry.

segments.source_text: I wouldn't allow social tensions to arise over basic daily needs.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ wʊdʌnt ʌlaʊ soʊʃʌl tɛntʃʌnz tuː ɝaɪz oʊvɝ beɪsɪk deɪliː niːds.
segments.maithili: हम wouldn't allow social tensions कें arise ऊपर basic daily needs.

segments.source_text: These core principles guided my approach. The entire world was in lockdown.
segments.source_ipa: ðiːz kɔːr prɪnsʌpʌlz ɡaɪdʌd maɪ əpprəətʃ. t̪ʰeː ɪntaɪɝ wɝld wɑːz ɪn ləkdoʊn.
segments.maithili: These core principles guided my approach. The entire world छल मे lockdown.

segments.source_text: Global economies were collapsing.
segments.source_ipa: ɡloʊbʌl ɪkɑːnʌmiːz wɝ cəlləpsəŋ.
segments.maithili: Global economies were collapsing.

segments.source_text: Everyone pressured me to empty the treasury, print more currency, and flood money everywhere.
segments.source_ipa: ɛvriːwʌn prɛʃɝd miː tuː ɛmptiː t̪ʰeː triːsəry, prɪnt mɔːr cərrəncy, ʌnd flʌd mʌniː əvərywərə.
segments.maithili: Everyone pressured me कें empty the treasury, print बेसी currency, आ flood money everywhere.

segments.source_text: But I decided that this was not the right economic route to follow.
segments.source_ipa: bʌt aɪ dɪsaɪdɪd ðæt ðɪs wɑːz nɑːt t̪ʰeː raɪt ɛkʌnɑːmɪk ruːt tuː fəlloʊ.
segments.maithili: मुदा हम decided ओ ई छल नहिं the right economic route कें follow.

segments.source_text: And so instead, the path I chose, after carefully listening to experts,
segments.source_ipa: ʌnd soʊ ənstiːd, t̪ʰeː pæθ aɪ tʃəsə, æftɝ kɛrfʌliː lɪsʌnɪŋ tuː əxpərts,
segments.maithili: आ so instead, the path हम chose, बाद carefully listening कें experts,

segments.source_text: understanding their opinions without opposing them,
segments.source_ipa: ʌndɝstændɪŋ ðɛr ʌpɪnjʌnz wɪθaʊt ʌpoʊzɪŋ ðəm,
segments.maithili: बुझब their opinions without opposing them,

segments.source_text: and combining their advice with my own country's situation
segments.source_ipa: ʌnd kʌmbaɪnɪŋ ðɛr ædvaɪs wɪð maɪ oʊn kʌntriːz sɪtʃuːeɪʃʌn
segments.maithili: आ combining their advice संग my own country's situation

segments.source_text: and my personal experiences, created a system that worked effectively.
segments.source_ipa: ʌnd maɪ pɝsɪnɪl əxpərəəncəs, kriːeɪtʌd ʌ sɪstʌm ðæt wɝkt əffəctəvəly.
segments.maithili: आ my personal experiences, created a तंत्र ओ worked effectively.

segments.source_text: As a result, when the whole world suffered from severe inflation immediately after COVID, India did not.
segments.source_ipa: æz ʌ rəsəlt, wɛn t̪ʰeː hoʊl wɝld sʌfɝd frʌm sʌvɪr ɪnfleɪʃʌn ɪmiːdiːʌtliː æftɝ cəvəd, ɪndiːʌ dɪd nət.
segments.maithili: As a result, when the whole world suffered सँ severe inflation immediately बाद COVID, India did not.

segments.source_text: Today, my country is steadily advancing at a rapid pace,
segments.source_ipa: tədeɪ, maɪ kʌntriː ɪz stɛdʌliː ʌdvænsɪŋ æt ʌ ræpʌd pəcə,
segments.maithili: Today, my country अछि steadily advancing at a rapid pace,

segments.source_text: emerging as one of the fastest-growing major economies in the world.
segments.source_ipa: ɪmɝdʒɪŋ æz wʌn ʌv t̪ʰeː fəstəst-groʊəŋ meɪdʒɝ ɪkɑːnʌmiːz ɪn t̪ʰeː wərld.
segments.maithili: emerging as एक केर the fastest-growing major economies मे the world.

segments.source_text: The main reason is that during that crisis, with patience and discipline,
segments.source_ipa: t̪ʰeː meɪn riːzʌn ɪz ðæt dʊrɪŋ ðæt crəsəs, wɪð peɪʃʌns ʌnd dəscəplənə,
segments.maithili: The main reason अछि ओ समयमे ओ crisis, संग patience आ discipline,

segments.source_text: I resisted the temptation to apply every global theory blindly.
segments.source_ipa: aɪ rɪzɪstɪd t̪ʰeː tɛmteɪʃʌn tuː ʌplaɪ ɛvɝiː ɡloʊbʌl θɪriː bləndly.
segments.maithili: हम resisted the temptation कें लागू करब every global theory blindly.

segments.source_text: We didn't worry about...
segments.source_ipa: wiː dɪdʌnt wɝiː əbaʊt...
segments.maithili: हम didn't worry about...
